name: Orgaz, Marjaliza Pass, and Los Yébenes Loop
name_original: Paso de Los Yébenes y Puerto de Marjaliza — circular desde Orgaz
description: Cycle a moderate 19.9-mile (32.1 km) circular route from Orgaz, tackling the scenic Paso de Los Yébenes and Puerto de Marjaliza in Montes de
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Orgaz are situated in the southern foothills of the Montes de Toledo, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features well-paved surfaces and varied landscapes, including undulating hills, cultivated fields, and olive groves. Cyclists can expect routes with both minimal elevation gain and more challenging climbs, providing options for different skill levels. The natural environment includes views of the Montes de Toledo and riverine landscapes approaching the Tagus River depression. Best road cycling routes around Orgaz The most popular road cycling route is Montes de Toledo – circular to Sierra del Castañar, a 62.9 miles (101.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through the Montes de Toledo. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Montes de Toledo: Sonseca, Mora, and Los Yébenes Loop, a moderate 36.7 miles (59.0 km) path. This route offers a mix of terrain through villages and agricultural fields. Local road cyclists also love the Consuegra, Urda y Puerto del Comendador — circular desde los Yébenes, a 61.3 miles (98.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with notable climbs, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Orgaz is defined by the Montes de Toledo foothills, cultivated fields, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for both easy rides and more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Orgaz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 280 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Orgaz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Orgaz?
A: The region around Orgaz, situated in the southern foothills of the Montes de Toledo, offers a diverse cycling landscape. You'll encounter undulating hills, cultivated fields of cereals and vineyards, and picturesque olive groves. Routes feature both sections with minimal elevation gain and more challenging climbs, providing a varied experience for different skill levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Orgaz offers options for easier rides. While many routes include significant climbs, there are 8 routes classified as easy. The terrain includes flat sections suitable for speed, alongside the more challenging climbs, allowing for a gradual introduction to the area's cycling.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the Orgaz area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025212009/montes-de-toledo-circular-to-sierra-del-castanar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo – circular to Sierra del Castañar&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, with over 860 meters of climbing over 101 kilometers. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366199793/consuegra-urda-y-puerto-del-comendador-circular-desde-los-yebenes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra, Urda y Puerto del Comendador — circular desde los Yébenes&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 830 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features and scenic views can I expect to see while cycling near Orgaz?
A: Cyclists can enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding natural environment, including vistas of the Montes de Toledo and riverine landscapes approaching the Tagus River depression. The routes often pass through typical Castilian agricultural fields and olive groves, offering a rich tapestry of rural Spanish scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore sites like the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Orgaz&lt;/a&gt;, a 14th-century fortress, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2127963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonacid Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orgaz&lt;/a&gt; itself offers a charming historic center with its Plaza Mayor and traditional arches.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Orgaz?
A: The road cycling routes around Orgaz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 280 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the well-paved surfaces, light traffic, and the blend of historical charm with diverse natural landscapes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Orgaz?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025216567/montes-de-toledo-sonseca-mora-and-los-yebenes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo: Sonseca, Mora, and Los Yébenes Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366262175/montes-de-toledo-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering engaging circular journeys through the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Orgaz?
A: The region's climate generally allows for enjoyable cycling for much of the year. However, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for long-distance rides and tackling climbs, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass through charming villages or offer cultural insights?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025216567/montes-de-toledo-sonseca-mora-and-los-yebenes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo: Sonseca, Mora, and Los Yébenes Loop&lt;/a&gt; take you through various villages and agricultural fields, offering glimpses into traditional Castilian life. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366262175/montes-de-toledo-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent choice for exploring the local culture and architecture.

Q: How many road cycling routes are there in total around Orgaz?
A: There are 22 road cycling routes available around Orgaz, offering a wide range of options for different distances and difficulty levels. This includes 8 easy routes and 14 moderate routes, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in the Orgaz area?
A: Yes, Orgaz is a great base for longer rides. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025212009/montes-de-toledo-circular-to-sierra-del-castanar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo – circular to Sierra del Castañar&lt;/a&gt; cover over 101 kilometers, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366199793/consuegra-urda-y-puerto-del-comendador-circular-desde-los-yebenes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuegra, Urda y Puerto del Comendador — circular desde los Yébenes&lt;/a&gt; is nearly 99 kilometers, providing ample opportunity for extended touring.
tips: The Castle of the Counts of Orgaz, is from the 14th century, possibly built on a previous one, it is a beautiful example of a residential fortress and of magnificent conservation. It is rectangular in shape and finished with beautiful battlements with marvelous cantilevered sentry boxes on the corners. The Torre del Homenaje has a semicircular apse corresponding to a chapel that breaks the rectilinear structure of the complex.
The Arch of San Huberto in Los Yébenes appears to be related to the Chapel of San Huberto, mentioned in local publications as part of the celebrations of the patron saint of hunters.
The Marjaliza Pass is located in the Montes de Toledo, within the Sierra de los Yébenes, and is very popular with cyclists due to its moderate difficulty and good road surface.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. It features two significant climbs: the Puerto de Los Yébenes (3.8 km, 4.5% average gradient) and the Puerto de Marjaliza (3.2 km, 5.9% average gradient). While the roads are well-maintained, the varying gradients on the climbs provide a good challenge, making it more suitable for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. You can expect generally good road conditions with light traffic, typical for the Montes de Toledo area. The climbs, while moderate, offer varying gradients, providing both a challenge and an enjoyable ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers marvellous views of the region and Montes de Toledo, especially from the summits of the passes. Notable highlights include the historical Castle of the Counts of Orgaz, the San Huberto Arch in Los Yébenes, and the scenic Marjaliza Pass. The town of Los Yébenes also features 16th-century churches and rich heritage.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montes de Toledo region is generally pleasant for cycling. Spring and autumn typically offer the most comfortable temperatures for tackling the climbs and enjoying the scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder conditions, so checking the weather forecast is always recommended.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route from Orgaz?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Orgaz. While specific parking details are not provided, as a circular route from a town, you should be able to find public parking within Orgaz to begin your ride. Look for designated parking areas or street parking available in the town center.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Montes de Toledo region?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads in the Montes de Toledo region, including this route. You are free to enjoy the natural beauty and historical sites without special permissions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You will encounter parts of the Camino Manchego de Santiago, the Cañada Real de Merinas, and the Risco de Los Yébenes, among others, during your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and the presence of two significant climbs, this route is generally not recommended for young children or beginner family cyclists. It's better suited for experienced riders or families with older, fit teenagers who are comfortable with road cycling challenges.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route for a break?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the towns of Orgaz and Los Yébenes. Both towns offer opportunities for breaks, where you can find cafes, restaurants, or shops to refuel and rest before continuing your journey.

faqs.question: What makes the climbs on this route notable?
faqs.answer: The climbs, particularly the Puerto de Los Yébenes, have a place in Spanish cycling history, having been featured in the Vuelta a España in 1974. Both the Puerto de Los Yébenes and the Puerto de Marjaliza offer beautiful slopes and are appreciated by cyclists for training and enjoying the scenic Montes de Toledo.

faqs.question: What kind of traffic can I expect on the roads?
faqs.answer: You can generally expect light traffic on the roads along this route. The Montes de Toledo area is known for its relatively quiet, well-maintained roads, making it a pleasant environment for road cycling.
